Title: Organ-delimited gene regulatory networks provide high accuracy in candidate transcription factor selection across diverse processes
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: seed lipids; total fatty acids; triacylglycerols
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed storage reserve; important food and biofuel trait
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in developing seeds and across multiple organs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: developing seeds; siliques during seed filling (11–13 DAP); also broadly expressed across organs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MybS2 was predicted by the seed organ-delimited GRN as a regulator of lipid biosynthesis and ranked 10th among candidate TFs. 2. Two independent T-DNA mutants (mybs2-1 and mybs2-2) showed reduced seed total fatty acid content, with mybs2-2 showing the largest reduction (~18%). 3. Complementation with pMybS2::MybS2 rescued the reduced fatty acid phenotype. 4. Overexpression of MybS2 (p35S::MybS2) increased total seed fatty acids and increased seed size and weight without obvious growth change. 5. RNA-seq and qRT-PCR showed that MybS2 induces PAP family genes and multiple lipid biosynthesis genes, supporting a positive regulatory role in seed lipid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes seed lipid accumulation and increases total fatty acid content, seed size, and seed weight
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional regulation of seed fatty acid and triacylglycerol biosynthesis and possibly reduced TAG breakdown
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAP7; PAP14; PAP23; PAP24; PAP25; LPCAT1; LPCAT2; PDHE1-α; KASI; FATA; FAD2; FAD3; SDP1; SDP1-like
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: induces PAP7/14/23/24/25 and multiple lipid biosynthesis genes including LPCAT1, LPCAT2, PDHE1-α, KASI, FATA, FAD2, FAD3; induces SDP1 and represses SDP1-like/SDPL1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA insertion mutants; complementation; constitutive overexpression; seed FAME quantification; seed size and weight phenotyping; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard growth room conditions; seed filling stage (11–13 DAP) for silique qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mutants had reduced seed size and weight; overexpression increased seed size and weight with no obvious growth-rate change reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising engineering target to increase seed oil content; orthologs in Brassica rapa and Camelina sativa are suggested as ready targets; may be combined with other lipid regulators
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: validated positive regulator with the strongest reduction among newly tested candidates; increases seed oil when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors propose a synergistic model in which MybS2 enhances FA synthesis via PAP genes and promotes TAG synthesis via lipid biosynthesis genes; exact direct targets and mechanism remain unresolved.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of MybS2-OE vs WT; organ-delimited RNA-seq meta-analysis; deposited in PURR (DOI: 10.4231/50R5-EM83)

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed storage reserve; important food and biofuel trait
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TGA4 was predicted by the seed-GRN as a candidate regulator of seed lipid biosynthesis. 2. A tga4 T-DNA mutant showed a significant reduction in total seed fatty acid content. 3. The study validates TGA4 as a positive regulator of seed lipid biosynthesis, but its mechanism was not elucidated.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes seed lipid accumulation; loss of function reduces total fatty acid content
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional regulation of seed lipid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA insertion mutant phenotyping; seed FAME quantification
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mutant also showed reduced seed weight and size according to candidate TF screen
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target for improving seed oil content in Arabidopsis and ortholog-guided engineering in Brassica oilseeds
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: newly validated positive regulator among top-ranked seed lipid TF candidates
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mode of action requires further experiments according to the authors.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: organ-delimited RNA-seq meta-analysis; deposited in PURR (DOI: 10.4231/50R5-EM83)

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed storage reserve; important food and biofuel trait
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPL12 was predicted by the seed-GRN as a candidate regulator of seed lipid biosynthesis. 2. A spl12 T-DNA mutant showed a significant reduction in total seed fatty acid content. 3. SPL12 was experimentally validated as a positive regulator of seed lipid biosynthesis, although the underlying mechanism was not resolved.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes seed lipid accumulation; mutation reduces total fatty acid content
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional regulation of seed lipid biosynthesis

Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mutant showed reduced seed weight and size in screen
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate TF for seed oil improvement and ortholog-based engineering in oilseed crops
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: newly validated positive regulator among top-ranked seed lipid TF candidates
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic basis was not elucidated in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: organ-delimited RNA-seq meta-analysis; deposited in PURR (DOI: 10.4231/50R5-EM83)
